testcase.name: asPythonValue > wraps a plain string in single quotes
asPythonValue > escapes a single quote inside a string
asPythonValue > escapes a backslash inside a string
asPythonValue > escapes backslash before single quote (both present)
asPythonValue > escapes newlines
asPythonValue > escapes carriage returns
asPythonValue > handles an empty string
asPythonValue > handles numbers as bare numeric literals
asPythonValue > handles booleans as Python True/False
asPythonValue > handles null as Python None
asPythonValue > produces output that contains no unmatched single quotes
socketServer.start() await semantics > fire-and-forget swallows synchronous errors (demonstrates the bug)
socketServer.start() await semantics > awaited start propagates errors (demonstrates the fix)
socketServer.start() await semantics > awaited start resolves on success
messageChunks > isComplete() returns false on a new instance
messageChunks > single chunk messages > isComplete() returns true after adding the single chunk
messageChunks > single chunk messages > fullMessage() returns exact buffer that was added
messageChunks > multi-chunk messages (ordered) > is not complete until all chunks are received
messageChunks > multi-chunk messages (ordered) > is complete after all chunks arrive in order
messageChunks > multi-chunk messages (ordered) > fullMessage() returns correct concatenation
messageChunks > multi-chunk messages (out-of-order) > is complete even when chunks arrive out of order
messageChunks > multi-chunk messages (out-of-order) > fullMessage() assembles in chunk-number order, not receive order
messageChunks > validation (error cases) > throws when adding a duplicate chunk with different header fields
messageChunks > validation (error cases) > throws when header chunkCount does not match constructor
messageChunks > validation (error cases) > throws when header messageLength does not match constructor
messageChunks > validation (error cases) > throws when declared chunkLength does not match buffer size
messageChunks > validation (error cases) > throws when chunk number is out of valid range
messageChunks > fullMessage() and idempotency > fullMessage() throws if called before all chunks are received
messageChunks > fullMessage() and idempotency > adding the same chunk twice is idempotent
messageChunks > integration: roundtrip > reassembles original payload split into multiple chunks
socketServer.close() > server starts and listens
socketServer.close() > close() stops listening
socketServer.close() > close() is idempotent
socketServer.close() > close() on never-started server is safe
protocol constants > constant HEADER_LENGTH equals 26 bytes
protocol constants > constant MAX_MESSAGE_SIZE equals 256 MB
protocol constants > hEADER_LENGTH is less than MAX_MESSAGE_SIZE
splitHeaderContentRest > buffer too small for header > returns Err when buffer is empty
splitHeaderContentRest > buffer too small for header > returns Err when buffer is shorter than HEADER_LENGTH
splitHeaderContentRest > buffer too small for header > error message mentions buffer and header lengths
splitHeaderContentRest > valid header — field round-trip > parses a header-only message (chunkLength = 0) correctly
splitHeaderContentRest > valid header — field round-trip > parses a message with chunk data and returns it in content
splitHeaderContentRest > valid header — field round-trip > returns trailing bytes as rest when buffer contains more than one message
splitHeaderContentRest > valid header — field round-trip > all custom field values round-trip through the header
splitHeaderContentRest > message size fields — server-side validation surface > parses a header where messageLength exceeds MAX_MESSAGE_SIZE (server must reject it)
splitHeaderContentRest > message size fields — server-side validation surface > parses a header where messageLength is exactly MAX_MESSAGE_SIZE
splitHeaderContentRest > message size fields — server-side validation surface > parses a header where messageLength equals HEADER_LENGTH (minimal valid)
splitHeaderContentRest > partial payload — full header but insufficient chunk data > returns Err when header declares chunkLength=16 but only 4 bytes of payload follow
socketServer integration — MAX_MESSAGE_SIZE rejection > destroys the client socket when messageLength exceeds MAX_MESSAGE_SIZE

failure.message: Test timed out in 5000ms. If this is a long-running test, pass a timeout value as the last argument or configure it globally with "testTimeout".

testcase.name: parsePythonResult — escaped strings > parses a plain single-quoted string
parsePythonResult — escaped strings > parses a string containing escaped double quotes (bug fix)
parsePythonResult — escaped strings > parses a string with an escaped backslash
parsePythonResult — escaped strings > parses a string with multiple escaped double quotes
parsePythonResult — escaped strings > parses a string with both backslash and double quote
parsePythonResult — escaped strings > still parses a plain string without escapes correctly
parsePythonResult — escaped strings > stripReprQuoting: outer Python repr-quoting is stripped > strips outer single quotes and parses the inner expression
parsePythonResult — escaped strings > stripReprQuoting: outer Python repr-quoting is stripped > strips outer double quotes and parses inner single-quoted string
parsePythonResult — escaped strings > integration: roundtrip with Python sanitize output > parses an empty string
parsePythonResult — escaped strings > integration: roundtrip with Python sanitize output > parses an Error response
savePathHelper — path traversal prevention (S7) > sanitization — variable names > preserves a normal variable name unchanged
savePathHelper — path traversal prevention (S7) > sanitization — variable names > preserves a short simple name
savePathHelper — path traversal prevention (S7) > sanitization — variable names > preserves alphanumeric names with underscores and digits
savePathHelper — path traversal prevention (S7) > path traversal — unix separators > blocks ../ traversal (stays inside saveDir)
savePathHelper — path traversal prevention (S7) > path traversal — unix separators > blocks ../../ traversal (stays inside saveDir)
savePathHelper — path traversal prevention (S7) > path traversal — unix separators > does not contain /etc/passwd after sanitizing ../../etc/passwd
savePathHelper — path traversal prevention (S7) > path traversal — windows backslash separators > blocks ..\ traversal (stays inside saveDir)
savePathHelper — path traversal prevention (S7) > path traversal — windows backslash separators > blocks ..\..\ traversal (stays inside saveDir)
savePathHelper — path traversal prevention (S7) > path traversal — bare dot sequences > blocks bare ".." variable (single-level traversal)
savePathHelper — path traversal prevention (S7) > path traversal — bare dot sequences > blocks single "." variable
savePathHelper — path traversal prevention (S7) > null byte injection > strips null bytes from variable names
savePathHelper — path traversal prevention (S7) > integration — savePathFor with variable > normal variable name produces path.join(saveDir, name)
savePathHelper — path traversal prevention (S7) > integration — savePathFor with variable > traversal variable does not escape saveDir
savePathHelper — path traversal prevention (S7) > integration — savePathFor with variable > expression objects use a short random id (not variable-based)
socketServer.onResponse — timeout (unit, fake timers) > starts with zero pending responses
socketServer.onResponse — timeout (unit, fake timers) > registers one pending response after onResponse is called
socketServer.onResponse — timeout (unit, fake timers) > fires onTimeout and cleans up the callback after timeout
socketServer.onResponse — timeout (unit, fake timers) > does not fire onTimeout when response arrives before timeout
socketServer.onResponse — timeout (unit, fake timers) > cleans up pending callback after successful response (no memory leak)
socketServer.onResponse — timeout (unit, fake timers) > callback is not called after timeout fires
socketServer.onResponse — integration (real server) > resolves callback when Python sends a real response over the socket

testcase.name: socketServer.onResponse — integration (real server) > starts with zero pending responses
smoke > framework is operational
smoke > typescript types work
socketServer — shared secret authentication (S4) > secretHex property > is a non-empty string
socketServer — shared secret authentication (S4) > secretHex property > has length AUTH_SECRET_LENGTH * 2
socketServer — shared secret authentication (S4) > secretHex property > contains only valid lowercase hex characters
socketServer — shared secret authentication (S4) > secretHex property > different SocketServer instances have different secrets
socketServer — shared secret authentication (S4) > secretHex property > returns the same secretHex on repeated accesses
socketServer — shared secret authentication (S4) > integration — connection auth > rejects connection with wrong secret (socket is destroyed)
socketServer — shared secret authentication (S4) > integration — connection auth > accepts connection with correct secret
socketServer — shared secret authentication (S4) > integration — connection auth > accepts connection when secret arrives in two fragments
socketServer — shared secret authentication (S4) > integration — connection auth > uses crypto.timingSafeEqual for comparison
assertNoTripleQuotes (triple-quote injection guard) > detects triple single quotes at start of string
assertNoTripleQuotes (triple-quote injection guard) > detects triple single quotes in the middle
assertNoTripleQuotes (triple-quote injection guard) > detects triple single quotes at end
assertNoTripleQuotes (triple-quote injection guard) > does not flag safe Python code
assertNoTripleQuotes (triple-quote injection guard) > does not flag content with only double quotes
assertNoTripleQuotes (triple-quote injection guard) > does not flag one single quote
assertNoTripleQuotes (triple-quote injection guard) > does not flag two consecutive single quotes
assertNoTripleQuotes (triple-quote injection guard) > does not flag mixed double and single quotes
assertNoTripleQuotes (triple-quote injection guard) > injection risk examples > attacker input that would break exec('''...''')
assertNoTripleQuotes (triple-quote injection guard) > injection risk examples > safe variable name is clean
